summary: A stellar 1973 matrix tape with crystal‑clear sound captures an electric show highlighted by a monumental Dark Star → Eyes of the World → China Doll sequence.
review: Listeners rave about the superb blend of audience ambience and board clarity that makes this matrix one of the finest recordings in the archive. The performance is fire‑filled, with Loose Lucy opening the night and the second‑set marathon of Dark Star, Eyes of the World and China Doll soaring to transcendental heights, punctuated by a standout Phil Lesh solo and Donna’s soulful take on “You Ain’t Woman Enough.”

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly five‑star praise for both the music and the matrix sound quality; only a single negative comment about track edits, yielding an average well above four stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Blend of audience mic and soundboard yields vivid, life‑like sound; reviewers note crispness and depth. Minor complaints about edited cue points (track cuts) but no impact on overall fidelity.

standout_songs: Dark Star → Eyes of the World → China Doll
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds energy, leading into a second‑set that reaches its peak with an extended Dark Star suite; transitions are praised for their seamless, improvisational flow (despite some edited cue points).

band_member_comments.Phil: Juicy Phil Lesh solo between Dark Star and Eyes of the World noted as a highlight.
